ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_instances" (
  "job_instance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job instance */,
  "job_name" TEXT /* Name of the job */,
  "job_key" TEXT /* Unique key for the job instance */,
  "job_type" TEXT /* Type of the job (e.g., data processing, report generation) */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job instance was created */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the job instance */,
  "job_configuration" TEXT /* Configuration settings for the job */,
  "job_priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the job */,
  "job_dependencies" TEXT /* JSON array of job instance IDs that this job depends on */,
  "job_tags" TEXT /* JSON array of tags for categorizing the job */,
  "job_version" INTEGER /* Version number of the job configuration */,
  "job_timeout" INTEGER /* Timeout duration for the job execution */,
  "job_notification_policy" TEXT /* Policy for notifications (e.g., always, on failure, on success) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job parameter */,
  "job_execution_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job execution the parameter belongs to */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter */,
  "parameter_type" TEXT /* Type of the parameter (e.g., string, integer, date) */,
  "identifying" INTEGER /* Indicator if the parameter is used to identify the job instance */,
  "parameter_description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "parameter_default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the parameter */,
  "parameter_mandatory" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the parameter is mandatory */,
  "parameter_encrypted" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the parameter value is encrypted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator, auditor) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "user_status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive, locked) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "user_contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  "user_last_password_reset" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last password reset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the job */,
  "job_instance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job instance being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the access event */,
  "access_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_access_logs_job_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_instance_id") REFERENCES job_instances ("job_instance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES job_users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "job_instance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job instance being audited */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the change */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the change */,
  "audit_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the audit event */,
  "audit_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the change was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES job_users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_audit_logs_job_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_instance_id") REFERENCES job_instances ("job_instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job schedule */,
  "job_instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job instance being scheduled */,
  "schedule_expression" TEXT /* Cron expression or other scheduling expression */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the schedule starts */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the schedule ends */,
  "last_run_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the last run */,
  "next_run_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the next run */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the schedule (e.g., active, paused, stopped) */,
  "schedule_description" TEXT /* Description of the schedule */,
  "schedule_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the schedule */,
  "schedule_next_retry_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the next retry if the schedule fails */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_schedules_job_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_instance_id") REFERENCES job_instances ("job_instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification setting */,
  "job_instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job instance the notification is for */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who will receive the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., success, failure, warning) */,
  "notification_channel" TEXT /* Channel of notification (e.g., email, SMS, push notification) */,
  "notification_threshold" INTEGER /* Threshold for triggering the notification (e.g., number of failures) */,
  "last_notification_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the last notification */,
  "notification_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the notification setting */,
  "notification_next_send_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the next notification send */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_notifications_job_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_instance_id") REFERENCES job_instances ("job_instance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES job_users ("user_id")
)
